item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this a lot

I echo a lot of some opinions here on the ending, it didn't feel too long to me perhaps, but it felt… sudden and out of left field I feel? And I am semi-disappointed with the lack of exploration for Xyank; their situation, and everything; but this is mostly because I was fascinated by the ideas of Detroit becoming metal; the idea of buildings of flesh, etc and I'm disappointed I didn't get to learn about what the other parasitic timelines were like; what drives them to become parasitic…

But? The concept? is very good, even if you don't explore it as in-depth as I'd want to in this, it offers me enough that makes me hunger for more and it makes me very happy so it gets me a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is a huge interesting idea, and I think you mostly pull it off

Couple of nitpicks:

- I think the ending note is too long, explains too much… I think you might want to condense it down to something like “what if anomalies were being shunted from one timeline to another” something more mysterious than spelling it all out

- “ Are these timelines actually parasitic? Dr Xyank seemed to suggest they were almost being fed upon by something. What it was more of a predator?”
Pretty sure you mean what if it was more of a predator… but also I think this is awkward phrasing, I think it would still work but with some tweaks

+1 either way :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Parasitic time lines: I like it! Reminds me of Doctor Who, where they mentioned "living timelines".

Though I do echo others: the ending does not exactly feel climatic.still, though, +1

Seconding a lot of the concerns already mentioned here.

The concept of parasitic timelines appeals to me, but the exploration of that concept here is weak. With Xyank's first transmission, you entice the reader with the excitement of a world-ending adventure. How did it happen? Who will survive? How will they deal with it? Then the first half of the offset is talking about parasitic timelines and what they are and how they work, but this is rather disconnected from Xyank's activities and the interesting story. Without that crucial contextualisation, reading your technobabble isn't very enjoyable. Xyank's message conveys to the reader that the world is fucked … in slightly more detail than before. I like the imagery of Dallas turning into metal, but there's not enough of this kind of thing to create a potent atmospheric piece of terror and awe. We get one more with the "buildings of flesh and bone melded into metal and plastic", which is a lovely aesthetic, but it's not quite enough. The third message explains how the Foundation received the messages a little bit and delivers more exposition but fails to develop the narrative in intriguing ways.

The final note is the most frustrating part. I get that you want a mysterious ending, but this feels incredibly forced. The ideas there aren't smoothly introduced or fully explored so this ending is not very satisfying. I was honestly really surprised to see that there wasn't another offset because this ends in an odd place where you haven't done a lot to make me care about these questions.

What little story this article has is spoon-fed to the reader in poorly formatted quoteblocks. A majority of it felt like I was reading through site lore buzzwords that were put together even before a full story was planned out. Overall, it just wasn't enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, dude. I’m not sure how I feel about this. I’m really enjoying this but it just doesn’t feel like a full story? Like it’s coming into something interesting and then it just ends.

The fact that anomalies are brought up so late in the game makes it seem less like a punchline and more like an interesting idea you just gave up on. I really want to like this but it just feels like the first act of a way more entertaining narrative.

-1 from me, sorry.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is an SCP that was initially written in a day but has undergone a lengthy crit process. After lots of tweaking and refining of the concept with huge help from my critters I decided to post it on my birthday, cause why not! Fingers crossed this has addressed all concerns and was an enjoyable read!

Huge thank you to all the critters listed below for their feedback:

  • OCuin
  • KaraKatt
  • vrkian
  • Veralta
  • Hexick
  • DianaBerry
  • BlueJones
  • TheSandwichMan
  • Doctor Fullham
  • not_a_seagull
  • cybersqyd
  • Anorrack
  • DrAkimoto
  • Sonderance
  • Greyve
